⭐ Health and Physical Education

Safety and First Aid

Learn how to stay safe and help others in emergencies!

📚 Unit 5 ⏱️ 11 min read

Unit 5: Safety and First Aid

Safety means protecting ourselves from harm. First aid is the immediate help given to an injured person before a doctor arrives.

Lesson 1: Safety Education

What is Safety?

Safety means being careful to prevent accidents and injuries.

Why is Safety Important?

ReasonExplanation
Prevents injuriesAccidents can cause pain and disability
Saves livesSome accidents are fatal
Reduces sufferingLess pain and suffering for victims
Saves moneyFewer hospital bills
Peace of mindYou feel secure and protected

Types of Safety

TypeWhereWhat to do
Home safetyAt homeKeep floors dry, store medicines safely
School safetyAt schoolWalk in corridors, do not run on stairs
Road safetyOn roadsFollow traffic rules
Playground safetyWhile playingUse proper equipment, do not push
Water safetyNear waterDo not swim alone, wear life jackets
Fire safetyDuring fireKnow emergency exits
Electrical safetyUsing electricityDo not touch wires with wet hands

Home Safety

HazardSafety rule
Wet floorsWipe spills immediately
StairsUse handrails, do not run
MedicinesKeep locked away from children
Sharp objects (knives, scissors)Keep out of reach
Hot water/gas stoveKeep handles inward
Electrical socketsUse socket covers

School Safety

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Walk in corridorsRun on stairs or corridors
Use the playground properlyPush or shove others
Report broken equipmentUse damaged swings or slides
Keep bags on racksLeave bags in walkways
Follow teacher’s instructionsClimb on desks or railings

Road Safety (Traffic Rules)

Golden Rules of Road Safety:

RuleExplanation
Look left-right-leftBefore crossing the road
Use zebra crossingCross only at crossings
Walk on the footpathDo not walk on the road
If no footpath, walk on the rightFacing oncoming traffic
Do not play on the roadRoads are for vehicles
Follow traffic lightsRed = stop, Yellow = wait, Green = go
Wear helmet on a bicycle/motorbikeProtects your head
Do not stick hands out of a moving busCan get hurt

Traffic Light Colors:

ColorMeaning
🔴 RedSTOP
🟡 YellowWAIT / GET READY
🟢 GreenGO

Playground Safety

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Warm up before playingStart playing suddenly
Use proper sports equipmentPlay with broken equipment
Wear sports shoesPlay in sandals or barefoot
Drink water during breaksPlay for hours without water
Follow game rulesPush, hit, or trip others

Water Safety

RuleWhy
Never swim aloneNo one to help if you drown
Learn swimmingKnow how to stay afloat
Wear a life jacketIn deep water or boats
Do not run near poolsWet floors are slippery
Do not dive in shallow waterCan hit your head

Fire Safety

What to do if there is a fire
Stay calm
Crawl low (under smoke)
Cover your nose with a wet cloth
Go to the nearest exit
Do not use the elevator
Call for help (101 in many countries)
Stop, drop, and roll if your clothes catch fire

Electrical Safety

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Use dry hands to plug/unplugTouch switches with wet hands
Pull the plug, not the wireYank the wire to remove it
Keep wires away from waterUse damaged wires or plugs
Turn off before repairingTouch electrical appliances with metal objects
Ask an adult for helpPut anything other than a plug into a socket

Lesson 2: First Aid

What is First Aid?

First aid is the immediate care given to an injured or sick person before professional medical help arrives.

Aims of First Aid (The 3 P’s)

AimMeaning
Preserve lifeKeep the person alive
Prevent worseningStop the injury from getting worse
Promote recoveryHelp the person get better

Contents of a First Aid Box

A first aid box should contain:

ItemUse
Sterile gauze and cottonClean wounds
Bandages (various sizes)Wrap injuries
Adhesive bandages (Band-Aids)Cover small cuts
Antiseptic liquid (Dettol, Savlon)Clean wounds
Antiseptic cream (Betadine)Apply on cuts
ScissorsCut bandages
TweezersRemove splinters
ThermometerCheck fever
Pain reliever (for adults only)Reduce pain
ORS packetsFor dehydration
GlovesProtect from germs

💡 Keep the first aid box in a clean, dry place that everyone knows about.

Common Injuries and First Aid

1. Cuts and Scrapes

StepWhat to do
1Wash your hands
2Clean the wound with clean water and antiseptic
3Remove any dirt or foreign objects (with tweezers)
4Apply antiseptic cream
5Cover with a sterile bandage or Band-Aid

2. Burns (Minor)

TypeFirst aid
Minor burn (red skin)Cool under running water for 10-15 minutes
Do NOT apply ice, butter, toothpaste, or oil
Cover with a clean cloth
Severe burnCall a doctor immediately

3. Nosebleed

Do ✅Don’t ❌
Sit up and lean slightly forwardTilt head backward
Pinch the soft part of the noseLie down
Apply a cold compress on the noseBlow the nose
Keep pinching for 10 minutesPut anything inside the nose

4. Sprain (Twisted ankle/wrist)

MethodMeaningAction
RRestDo not move the injured part
IIceApply ice pack (wrapped in cloth) for 15-20 min
CCompressionWrap with an elastic bandage
EElevationRaise the injured part above heart level

💡 RICE method is for sprains and strains.

5. Insect Bite or Sting

StepWhat to do
1Remove the stinger (if bee sting) by scraping
2Wash with soap and water
3Apply cold compress
4Apply calamine lotion for itching
5See a doctor if allergic reaction occurs

6. Fracture (Broken Bone)

SignWhat to do
Pain, swelling, cannot move the partDo not move the person
Do not try to straighten the bone
Support the injured part with a splint
Apply ice (wrapped in cloth)
Call for medical help

7. Choking

SignWhat to do
Cannot speak, cough, or breatheFor children: Give back blows
For adults: Heimlich maneuver (abdominal thrusts)
Call for help immediately

8. Dog Bite or Animal Bite

StepWhat to do
1Wash the wound with soap and water for 15 minutes
2Apply antiseptic
3Cover with a clean cloth
4Go to a doctor for rabies vaccine

9. Poisoning

If someone swallows poison
Do NOT make them vomit (except if doctor says)
Call a poison control center or doctor immediately
Keep the poison container to show the doctor

10. Fainting

StepWhat to do
1Lay the person down
2Raise their legs slightly (above heart level)
3Loosen tight clothing
4Provide fresh air
5Do not give water until fully conscious

Emergency Phone Numbers

EmergencyPhone Number (in Nepal)
Police100
Ambulance102
Fire Brigade101
Child Helpline1098

(Note: These numbers may vary by country. Learn your local emergency numbers.)

💡 Remember: The most important first aid step is to stay calm and call for help if needed.

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. Safety means protecting ourselves from ______.
  2. When crossing the road, we should look ______, ______, ______.
  3. A ______ box contains first aid supplies.
  4. The RICE method is for treating a ______.
  5. For a minor burn, cool the area under ______ water.

Exercise 2: True or False

  1. We should run on stairs at school. (T/F)
  2. A first aid box should contain scissors and bandages. (T/F)
  3. For a nosebleed, tilt the head backward. (T/F)
  4. We should play on the road. (T/F)
  5. The Heimlich maneuver is for choking. (T/F)

Exercise 3: Match the First Aid to the Injury

InjuryFirst Aid
Cuta) RICE method
Sprainb) Cool under running water
Minor burnc) Clean and cover with bandage
Nosebleedd) Pinch nose and lean forward

Exercise 4: What Would You Do?

Write the first aid step for each situation.

  1. Your friend falls and gets a deep cut on the knee. → ____________
  2. Your little brother touches a hot pan and burns his finger. → ____________
  3. Someone is choking and cannot breathe. → ____________

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. harm/injury, 2. left, right, left; 3. first aid, 4. sprain, 5. running

Exercise 2: 1. F, 2. T, 3. F, 4. F, 5. T

Exercise 3: Cut→c, Sprain→a, Minor burn→b, Nosebleed→d

Great work! Safety first, and always be ready to help others in need!
